source:
# ipywidgets Gallery

A tour of interactive widgets. Run each cell (Shift+Enter) to see the widget, then try dragging / typing / clicking.

## Sliders

`IntSlider` and `FloatSlider` — the bread-and-butter interactive inputs.

int_slider = widgets.IntSlider(value=5, min=0, max=20, step=1, description='int')
float_slider = widgets.FloatSlider(value=1.5, min=0.0, max=10.0, step=0.1, description='float')
widgets.VBox([int_slider, float_slider])

## Text input

Typed input — integers, floats, free-form strings. The `Textarea` allows multi-line input.

widgets.VBox([
    widgets.IntText(value=42, description='int'),
    widgets.FloatText(value=3.14, description='float'),
    widgets.Text(value='hello', placeholder='type here', description='text'),
    widgets.Textarea(value='line 1\nline 2', description='area'),

## Choices

Pick one from a set — `Dropdown`, `RadioButtons`, or `Select`.

options = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry', 'durian']
widgets.VBox([
    widgets.Dropdown(options=options, description='dropdown'),
    widgets.RadioButtons(options=options, description='radio'),
    widgets.Select(options=options, description='select'),

## Toggles & buttons

Boolean controls and push buttons.

widgets.VBox([
    widgets.Checkbox(value=True, description='checkbox'),
    widgets.ToggleButton(value=False, description='toggle'),
    widgets.ToggleButtons(options=['cold', 'warm', 'hot'], description='3-way'),
    widgets.Button(description='click me', button_style='primary'),

## Button click handler

A button wired up to an `Output` widget. Clicking appends a log line.

btn = widgets.Button(description='Add log line', icon='plus')
log = widgets.Output()

counter = {'n': 0}
def on_click(_):
    counter['n'] += 1
    with log:
        print(f'clicked {counter["n"]} time(s)')
btn.on_click(on_click)

widgets.VBox([btn, log])

import datetime
widgets.VBox([
    widgets.DatePicker(value=datetime.date.today(), description='date'),
    widgets.ColorPicker(value='#2196f3', description='color'),

## `interact` decorator

Shortest path from a function to a widget — `@interact` auto-creates controls for every argument.

## Layout: HBox / VBox / Accordion / Tab

Combine widgets into panels.

tab_contents = [
    widgets.VBox([widgets.IntSlider(description=f'slider {i}') for i in range(3)]),
    widgets.HBox([widgets.Button(description=str(i)) for i in range(5)]),
    widgets.Textarea(value='write notes here...'),
]
tab = widgets.Tab(children=tab_contents)
tab.set_title(0, 'Sliders')
tab.set_title(1, 'Buttons')
tab.set_title(2, 'Notes')

acc = widgets.Accordion(children=[
    widgets.Text(description='name'),
    widgets.IntRangeSlider(value=[25, 75], min=0, max=100, description='range'),
])
acc.set_title(0, 'Details')
acc.set_title(1, 'Preferences')

widgets.VBox([tab, acc])
